display.purpose: Giant content titles and cinematic displays.

motion.name: instant
motion.purpose: Immediate state changes with no transitions or animations.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: High importance main CTA buttons like Play or Download.
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { background: #ebd29f; color: #2e3335; padding: 10px 20px; font-weight: 700; border: none; border-radius: calc(var(--radius) - 2px); cursor: pointer; transition: none; }

narrative.overview: CaféVerse is a premium, high-performance desktop movie and TV show catalog workspace. Built on the core principle of 'The Obsidian Theater,' the design treats the UI as a subtle, darkened auditorium. UI panels, controls, and borders melt gracefully into the shadows, ensuring that vibrant media cover posters and movie backdrops stand as the sole glowing hero on the screen.
keyCharacteristics: Obsidian Dimmed Canvas: Immersive, flat-dark surfaces with extremely subtle light offsets.
Warm Sand Accents: Soft, golden-amber primary highlights that represent a single, active color highlight.
Zero Motion Overhead: Instant, direct responses with absolute 0-animation overhead.
Content is Hero: High-contrast, elegant typography combined with borderless media.

rules.name: The Content-First Ratio
rules.body: Warm sand primary color must occupy <=10% of any given screen area. The absolute dark obsidian background must carry the remaining weight to preserve cinematic night legibility.

rules.name: The Flat Obsidian Doctrine
rules.body: Shadows are forbidden. Depth is achieved entirely through background contrast or border-tint overlays rather than blurred drop shadows.

dos: Do let the cinematic poster art carry the visual excitement of the page.
Do keep components flat and borderless, relying on tone-on-tone layouts.
Do respect the 0-animation standard, ensuring immediate state toggles with no layout shifts.
Do tint all dividers and borders with the warm sand hue to match the color strategy.
donts: Don't use SaaS cliché card grids with identical card sizes and heavy borders.
Don't use bright neon or overly saturated colors for interactive borders or accents.
Don't introduce generic or flat, boring list tables.
Don't use standard box shadows to floatingly separate UI panels.
